en_definition: unwilling or disinclined to do something
example1: She was reluctant to accept the job offer initially.
example1_ru: Сначала она неохотно приняла предложение о работе.
example2: Many students are reluctant to participate in group presentations.

en_definition: done consciously and intentionally, not accidental
example1: The deliberate choice to skip the class had serious consequences.
example1_ru: Осознанное решение пропустить занятие имело серьёзные последствия.
example2: His deliberate silence was interpreted as disagreement.

en_definition: really what it appears to be; authentic or sincere
example1: Her genuine interest in learning impressed all her teachers.
example1_ru: Её искренний интерес к учёбе произвёл впечатление на всех учителей.
example2: This is a genuine artifact, not a modern replica.

en_definition: satisfactory or enough for a particular purpose
example1: The funding was adequate for our research needs.
example1_ru: Финансирования было достаточно для наших исследовательских нужд.
example2: Your preparation is barely adequate for this examination.

en_definition: certain to happen; impossible to avoid or prevent
example1: Climate change has inevitable consequences for ecosystems worldwide.
example1_ru: Изменение климата влечёт за собой неизбежные последствия для экосистем во всём мире.
example2: Growing older is an inevitable part of human experience.

en_definition: a belief, idea, or conception about something
example1: The notion that success comes easily is incorrect.
example1_ru: Представление о том, что успех даётся легко, ошибочно.
example2: Popular notions about intelligence have changed over time.

en_definition: prejudice or unfair preference for one thing over another
example1: Confirmation bias affects how we interpret evidence.
example1_ru: Склонность к подтверждению своей точки зрения влияет на то, как мы интерпретируем факты.
example2: The survey showed gender bias in hiring decisions.

en_definition: delicate, slight, and not immediately noticeable
example1: The subtle differences between the two paintings required close examination.
example1_ru: Тонкие различия между двумя картинами требовали внимательного изучения.
example2: There is a subtle shift in the author's tone throughout the book.

en_definition: noticeably different or clearly defined
example1: Each student has distinct learning preferences and styles.
example1_ru: У каждого студента свои чёткие учебные предпочтения и стиль обучения.
example2: The two groups had distinct characteristics and needs.

en_definition: expressed clearly and easy to understand
example1: Her lucid explanation helped the whole class understand the topic.
example1_ru: Её ясное объяснение помогло всему классу разобраться в теме.
example2: The report gives a lucid summary of the company's finances.

en_definition: not clearly expressed or defined, so that the meaning is uncertain
example1: His vague answer left the interviewer with more questions.
example1_ru: Его расплывчатый ответ оставил у интервьюера ещё больше вопросов.
example2: The instructions were too vague for the new employees to follow.

en_definition: possible to do or achieve; practical
example1: A three-year timeline is feasible for this project.
example1_ru: Трёхлетний срок для этого проекта вполне осуществим.
example2: Is this solution feasible given our budget constraints?

en_definition: complete and covering all relevant aspects
example1: The textbook provides a comprehensive overview of the subject.
example1_ru: Учебник даёт всеобъемлющий обзор предмета.
example2: A comprehensive approach addresses all parts of the problem.

en_definition: of great importance; critical to achieving something
example1: Clear communication is crucial for team success.
example1_ru: Чёткая коммуникация имеет решающее значение для успеха команды.
example2: The timing of this decision is crucial for the outcome.

en_definition: very different from each other; varied
example1: Our class has diverse backgrounds and experiences.
example1_ru: У нашего класса разнообразный состав по происхождению и опыту.
example2: The ecosystem supports diverse plant and animal species.

en_definition: not influenced by personal feelings or opinions; factual
example1: An objective observer would notice the obvious bias.
example1_ru: Объективный наблюдатель заметил бы очевидную предвзятость.
example2: Scientific research requires objective measurements.

en_definition: having the skill or ability to do something
example1: She is quite capable of completing this task independently.
example1_ru: Она вполне способна выполнить эту задачу самостоятельно.
example2: Modern technology is capable of processing vast amounts of data.

en_definition: an inclination to do something; a general direction or trend
example1: There is a tendency for students to procrastinate before exams.
example1_ru: Студенты склонны откладывать дела перед экзаменами.
example2: Recent research shows a tendency toward more remote work.

en_definition: a clear understanding or realization about something
example1: This research provides valuable insight into human behavior.
example1_ru: Это исследование даёт ценное понимание человеческого поведения.
example2: Her insight helped us solve the difficult problem.

en_definition: to notice or understand something that is not immediately obvious
example1: It was hard to discern any pattern in the scattered data.
example1_ru: В разрозненных данных было трудно распознать какую-либо закономерность.
example2: Experienced doctors can discern subtle symptoms that others miss.

en_definition: to get or obtain something through effort
example1: Students acquire knowledge through active learning.
example1_ru: Студенты приобретают знания через активное обучение.
example2: You can acquire new skills through practice.

en_definition: to maintain or support something over time
example1: We need to sustain our motivation throughout the project.
example1_ru: Нам нужно сохранять мотивацию на протяжении всего проекта.
example2: The environment cannot sustain this level of pollution.

en_definition: to experience or endure something
example1: Students undergo significant changes during university years.
example1_ru: Студенты претерпевают значительные изменения за годы учёбы в университете.
example2: The material undergoes chemical transformation when heated.

en_definition: to meet or come across someone or something
example1: Researchers encounter unexpected challenges during field work.
example1_ru: Исследователи сталкиваются с неожиданными трудностями во время полевой работы.
example2: Did you encounter any problems with the assignment?

en_definition: to commit oneself or something to a particular purpose or activity
example1: She dedicated her life to teaching underprivileged children.
example1_ru: Она посвятила свою жизнь обучению детей из малообеспеченных семей.
example2: Much time is dedicated to group discussion in this course.

en_definition: to develop something in detail; to explain more fully
example1: Can you elaborate on your previous statement?
example1_ru: Не могли бы вы подробнее раскрыть своё предыдущее утверждение?
example2: The author elaborates on the theory in later chapters.

en_definition: to reduce in size or to express something in fewer words
example1: Please condense your report to five pages maximum.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, сократите отчёт максимум до пяти страниц.
example2: The information was condensed into a summary table.

en_definition: to involve something as a necessary part or result
example1: The new role will entail frequent travel to other offices.
example1_ru: Новая должность будет предполагать частые поездки в другие офисы.
example2: Starting a business can entail significant financial risk.

en_definition: to hide or keep something secret from others
example1: The report concealed important information about the incident.
example1_ru: В отчёте были скрыты важные сведения об инциденте.
example2: It is difficult to conceal one's emotions completely.

en_definition: to make something known; to show something hidden
example1: The study reveals surprising patterns in the data.
example1_ru: Исследование выявляет удивительные закономерности в данных.
example2: Testing will reveal whether the hypothesis is correct.

en_definition: to accept as true without proof; to take for granted
example1: Do not assume all students have access to computers.
example1_ru: Не следует предполагать, что у всех студентов есть доступ к компьютерам.
example2: Many researchers assume the data is normally distributed.

en_definition: to think or assume something is likely or probable
example1: I presume you have read the assigned chapter.
example1_ru: Полагаю, вы прочитали заданную главу.
example2: We must presume innocence until proven guilty.

en_definition: to ignore or pay no attention to something
example1: The report disregards important contextual factors.
example1_ru: В отчёте не учитываются важные контекстуальные факторы.
example2: Safety protocols must never be disregarded.

en_definition: to admit or accept that something is true
example1: The authors acknowledge the limitations of their study.
example1_ru: Авторы признают ограничения своего исследования.
example2: We must acknowledge both strengths and weaknesses.

en_definition: to agree to something or give permission
example1: Participants must consent to the research procedures.
example1_ru: Участники должны дать согласие на проведение исследовательских процедур.
example2: She did not consent to sharing her personal information.

en_definition: to behave according to accepted standards, rules, or expectations
example1: All products must conform to strict safety regulations.
example1_ru: Все изделия должны соответствовать строгим требованиям безопасности.
example2: She refused to conform to the traditional dress code at work.

en_definition: to adjust or provide what is needed for someone
example1: The university accommodates students with various disabilities.
example1_ru: Университет создаёт условия для студентов с различными видами инвалидности.
example2: We accommodated the guest's dietary preferences.

en_definition: to make compatible or consistent; to resolve differences
example1: We must reconcile these conflicting research findings.
example1_ru: Нам нужно примирить эти противоречащие друг другу результаты исследований.
example2: Theory and practice are sometimes difficult to reconcile.

en_definition: to give something to balance or make up for something else
example1: The bonus compensated for the earlier pay reduction.
example1_ru: Премия компенсировала предыдущее снижение зарплаты.
example2: We compensated for the missing data by interpolating.

en_definition: to become worse or lower in quality
example1: Air quality deteriorated significantly during winter months.
example1_ru: Качество воздуха значительно ухудшилось в зимние месяцы.
example2: Without maintenance, the building will deteriorate quickly.

en_definition: to be successful and flourish; to thrive
example1: Businesses prosper when they adapt to market changes.
example1_ru: Бизнес процветает, когда адаптируется к изменениям рынка.
example2: Students prosper with consistent effort and good support.

en_definition: to be widespread; to win or succeed in a conflict
example1: Common sense prevails in most educational decisions.
example1_ru: В большинстве образовательных решений преобладает здравый смысл.
example2: The team's determination prevailed over adversity.

en_definition: to find out or determine something with certainty
example1: We need to ascertain the causes of the problem.
example1_ru: Нам нужно установить причины проблемы.
example2: Research ascertained that diet affects student performance.

en_definition: to examine something very carefully and thoroughly
example1: Experts scrutinized the original documents for authenticity.
example1_ru: Эксперты тщательно изучили подлинные документы на предмет подлинности.
example2: The committee scrutinized the budget proposal in detail.

en_definition: to investigate or question something thoroughly
example1: The inquiry probed into the causes of the failure.
example1_ru: Расследование углубилось в причины сбоя.
example2: Scientists probe the mysteries of the deep ocean.

ru_translation: экстраполировать, делать вывод на основе данных
en_definition: to estimate an unknown value or trend based on known information
example1: Scientists extrapolate future climate patterns from past data.
example1_ru: Учёные экстраполируют будущие климатические тенденции на основе прошлых данных.
example2: It is risky to extrapolate too much from a small sample.

en_definition: to interpret the meaning of something
example1: Her silence was construed as agreement by some.
example1_ru: Её молчание некоторые истолковали как согласие.
example2: How should we construe this ambiguous statement?

en_definition: to refer to something indirectly or in passing
example1: The author alludes to historical events without naming them.
example1_ru: Автор намекает на исторические события, не называя их напрямую.
example2: He alluded to problems in his marriage but said nothing specific.

en_definition: to give or grant something; to agree or be in harmony
example1: The principle accorded respect to all students equally.
example1_ru: Принцип предоставлял уважение всем студентам в равной степени.
example2: Our findings accord with previous research in the field.

en_definition: to give or do something in return for something received
example1: We appreciate your help and reciprocate the gesture.
example1_ru: Мы ценим вашу помощь и хотим ответить взаимностью.
example2: Did she reciprocate your feelings for her?

en_definition: disagreement or holding a different opinion from others
example1: There was considerable dissent within the research team.
example1_ru: В исследовательской группе наблюдались значительные разногласия.
example2: The minority opinion represents legitimate scientific dissent.

en_definition: general agreement reached by a group of people about an issue
example1: The committee finally reached a consensus after a long debate.
example1_ru: Комитет наконец пришёл к согласию после долгих дебатов.
example2: There is no scientific consensus on the exact cause yet.

en_definition: an event or circumstance that comes before and may influence a later one
example1: Historians study the antecedent causes of the economic crisis.
example1_ru: Историки изучают предшествующие причины экономического кризиса.
example2: The report explains the antecedent conditions that led to the strike.

en_definition: a confusing and difficult problem that is hard to solve
example1: The team faced a real conundrum when both plans seemed flawed.
example1_ru: Команда столкнулась с настоящей дилеммой, когда оба плана показались несовершенными.
example2: Balancing cost and quality remains a conundrum for the factory.

en_definition: something that does not follow the usual pattern or rule
example1: Auditors found an irregularity in the company's financial records.
example1_ru: Аудиторы обнаружили нарушение в финансовой отчётности компании.
example2: The doctor noticed an irregularity in the patient's heartbeat.

en_definition: a great difference or inequality between things
example1: There is a significant disparity in funding between schools.
example1_ru: Наблюдается значительное неравенство в финансировании между школами.
example2: Gender disparities in education persist in many countries.

en_definition: the state of having mixed or contradictory feelings
example1: Students often feel ambivalence about moving to university.
example1_ru: Студенты часто испытывают двойственные чувства по поводу поступления в университет.
example2: Her ambivalence about the decision delayed action.

en_definition: the size or extent to which something affects a situation
example1: Experts are still measuring the scale of impact of the storm.
example1_ru: Эксперты всё ещё оценивают масштаб последствий урагана.
example2: The report describes the scale of impact on local wildlife.

en_definition: a strong desire to achieve something or succeed
example1: Her ambition to become a scientist drives her studies.
example1_ru: Её стремление стать учёным движет её учёбой.
example2: Ambition is admirable but needs to be realistic.

en_definition: the ability to do something well; skill or expertise
example1: Teaching requires both knowledge and pedagogical competence.
example1_ru: Преподавание требует как знаний, так и педагогической компетентности.
example2: His competence in mathematics is clearly demonstrated.

en_definition: a situation requiring a choice between equally difficult options
example1: The professor faces a dilemma between fairness and efficiency.
example1_ru: Профессор сталкивается с дилеммой между справедливостью и эффективностью.
example2: This dilemma has no perfect solution.

en_definition: the strength or speed of growth or movement; power of progress
example1: The project gained momentum after the successful first phase.
example1_ru: Проект набрал обороты после успешного первого этапа.
example2: Physics defines momentum as mass times velocity.

en_definition: something that discourages or prevents action
example1: Strict penalties serve as a deterrent to plagiarism.
example1_ru: Строгие наказания служат сдерживающим фактором против плагиата.
example2: High costs can be a deterrent to adopting new technology.

en_definition: something that causes or speeds up change or action
example1: Crisis often serves as a catalyst for innovation.
example1_ru: Кризис часто становится катализатором инноваций.
example2: In chemistry, a catalyst speeds reactions without being consumed.

en_definition: a possible situation or set of circumstances
example1: Consider several scenarios before making your decision.
example1_ru: Прежде чем принять решение, рассмотрите несколько сценариев.
example2: The worst-case scenario would be complete system failure.

en_definition: a false reason given to hide the real purpose
example1: He used illness as a pretext to avoid the meeting.
example1_ru: Он использовал болезнь как предлог, чтобы не идти на встречу.
example2: The investigation revealed the excuse was merely a pretext.

en_definition: the time between two events; a temporary period
example1: In the interim, we will develop preliminary plans.
example1_ru: На переходный период мы разработаем предварительные планы.
example2: An interim report was submitted in June.

en_definition: a critical point or moment; the place where things meet
example1: At this juncture, we must decide our future direction.
example1_ru: На данном этапе мы должны определить наше дальнейшее направление.
example2: The juncture of the two rivers creates a unique ecosystem.

en_definition: a widely accepted way of behaving or doing something
example1: Scientific papers follow strict conventions for citing sources.
example1_ru: Научные статьи следуют строгим правилам цитирования источников.
example2: Breaking social convention can sometimes lead to new ideas.

en_definition: moral excellence; a good quality or advantage
example1: Honesty is an important virtue in research.
example1_ru: Честность — важное достоинство в исследовательской работе.
example2: By virtue of her experience, she was selected.

en_definition: a bad habit or moral fault; an evil or wicked behavior
example1: Laziness is a vice that hinders academic success.
example1_ru: Лень — порок, мешающий академическому успеху.
example2: Procrastination is a common vice among students.

en_definition: worth or quality of something; something deserving reward
example1: The proposal has considerable merit and should be considered.
example1_ru: У этого предложения есть немалые достоинства, и его стоит рассмотреть.
example2: Jobs should be assigned on merit, not connections.

en_definition: a disadvantage or negative aspect of something
example1: A major drawback of online learning is lack of interaction.
example1_ru: Существенный недостаток онлайн-обучения — отсутствие живого общения.
example2: Cost is the main drawback of this technology.

en_definition: something that motivates or encourages someone to act
example1: Financial incentives motivated employees to improve performance.
example1_ru: Финансовые стимулы побудили сотрудников улучшить показатели.
example2: What incentives will encourage student participation?

en_definition: something that hinders or prevents progress
example1: Language barriers are an impediment to communication.
example1_ru: Языковые барьеры служат препятствием для общения.
example2: Legal impediments may slow the project.

en_definition: one aspect or face of something having many sides
example1: Each facet of the problem requires careful analysis.
example1_ru: Каждая грань этой проблемы требует тщательного анализа.
example2: The facets of a gemstone sparkle in light.

en_definition: priority or right to come before others in order
example1: Student safety takes precedence over all other concerns.
example1_ru: Безопасность студентов имеет приоритет над всеми остальными соображениями.
example2: Does research take precedence over teaching?

en_definition: the process of changing from one state to another
example1: The transition from school to university is challenging.
example1_ru: Переход от школы к университету даётся нелегко.
example2: Economic transitions often create social strain.

en_definition: something that must be done first as a requirement
example1: Mathematics is a prerequisite for advanced physics.
example1_ru: Математика — обязательное условие для изучения продвинутой физики.
example2: Prior experience is not a prerequisite for this program.

en_definition: to try hard to achieve something
example1: We endeavor to provide the best education possible.
example1_ru: Мы стремимся предоставить наилучшее возможное образование.
example2: Scientists endeavor to understand natural phenomena.

en_definition: to continue doing something despite difficulty
example1: Successful students persevere through challenges.
example1_ru: Успешные студенты упорно продолжают, несмотря на трудности.
example2: Athletes must persevere to achieve their goals.

en_definition: to change or improve something; to study again
example1: Please revise your essay before submission.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, доработайте своё эссе перед сдачей.
example2: I revised my opinion after hearing new evidence.

en_definition: a note at the bottom of a page giving extra information or a source
example1: The author added a footnote to explain the unusual term.
example1_ru: Автор добавил сноску, чтобы объяснить непривычный термин.
example2: Check the footnote for the original source of this quote.

en_definition: to refer to or appeal to something for support
example1: The speaker invoked historical examples to support the argument.
example1_ru: Докладчик сослался на исторические примеры в поддержку своего аргумента.
example2: She invoked the principle of justice.

en_definition: to suggest or assume something as a basis for reasoning
example1: The theory postulates three basic principles.
example1_ru: Теория постулирует три базовых принципа.
example2: We postulate that student engagement improves results.

en_definition: to strengthen or provide support for something
example1: New evidence bolsters the original hypothesis.
example1_ru: Новые данные подкрепляют исходную гипотезу.
example2: Good planning bolsters team confidence.

en_definition: to gradually weaken or damage something
example1: Inconsistency undermines the credibility of the argument.
example1_ru: Непоследовательность подрывает достоверность аргумента.
example2: Poor communication undermines team effectiveness.

en_definition: to begin a journey or undertake something new
example1: Students embark on their university education with hope.
example1_ru: Студенты начинают своё университетское образование с надеждой.
example2: We are embarking on a new research project.

en_definition: to combine things to form a single stronger whole
example1: We consolidated our findings into a comprehensive report.
example1_ru: Мы объединили наши выводы в один всесторонний отчёт.
example2: The company consolidated its various departments.

en_definition: to make one's control or position stronger and more secure
example1: The new leader worked quickly to consolidate power in the region.
example1_ru: Новый лидер быстро принялся укреплять власть в регионе.
example2: The company tried to consolidate power within its industry.

en_definition: to light up; to make something clear or easy to understand
example1: This research illuminates an important gap in knowledge.
example1_ru: Это исследование проливает свет на важный пробел в знаниях.
example2: A clear diagram illuminates the complex process.

en_definition: to make unclear or difficult to see or understand
example1: Technical jargon obscures the meaning for general readers.
example1_ru: Техническая терминология затемняет смысл для рядовых читателей.
example2: The true cause remains obscured by conflicting evidence.

en_definition: to show or prove that someone or something is correct
example1: Recent research vindicates our original theory.
example1_ru: Недавние исследования подтверждают нашу первоначальную теорию.
example2: The investigation vindicated him of all charges.

en_definition: to formally disagree with or challenge a statement someone has made
example1: The scientist chose to dispute a claim made in the article.
example1_ru: Учёный решил оспорить утверждение, сделанное в статье.
example2: Lawyers often dispute a claim before it reaches court.

en_definition: to argue against or respond to a claim with a counter-argument
example1: The team rebutted the criticism with new data.
example1_ru: Команда опровергла критику новыми данными.
example2: She rebutted his claims point by point.

en_definition: to restore someone or something to a former position
example1: The court reinstated the original policy decision.
example1_ru: Суд восстановил первоначальное политическое решение.
example2: We reintroduced the discontinued program last year.

en_definition: to use something to gain an advantage or support
example1: We can leverage existing resources for the new project.
example1_ru: Мы можем использовать имеющиеся ресурсы для нового проекта.
example2: Social media can leverage public opinion.

en_definition: to make a process simpler and more efficient by removing unnecessary steps
example1: The new software will streamline how orders are processed.
example1_ru: Новое программное обеспечение упростит обработку заказов.
example2: Managers hope to streamline communication between departments across the company.

en_definition: to control or limit something, especially something harmful
example1: The city introduced new rules to curb air pollution.
example1_ru: Город ввёл новые правила, чтобы сдержать загрязнение воздуха.
example2: Doctors advised him to curb his intake of sugar and salt.

en_definition: to increase rapidly in number; to multiply
example1: Online learning platforms have proliferated recently.
example1_ru: Платформы онлайн-обучения в последнее время стремительно распространились.
example2: Species proliferate in favorable environmental conditions.
